fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer occupational therapist license to georgia, occupational therapist compact georgia, georgia occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Georgia Board of Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address: Professional Licensing Boards Division, 237 Coliseum Drive, Macon, Georgia 31217-3858

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Georgia.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal, select 'New Application' for Occupational Therapy, and either create a new user account or log in to begin the online application form.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within the Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal, providing personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents, including official transcripts from your accredited OT program, verification of supervised fieldwork, and your NBCOT exam score report. Complete a criminal background check as required.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee of $100 through the online portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your account on the Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal to check the status of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Georgia offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Georgia Board of Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Georgia is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Georgia without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Georgia also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $60. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Georgia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Georgia is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Georgia without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Georgia also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Georgia, submit your application through the Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal.

official_links.portal_name: Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Georgia SOS Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal, select 'New Application', then choose 'Occupational Therapy' and 'Occupational Therapist'. You will be prompted to create a new user account if you do not already have one, or log in to proceed.
